sources: SF MEDDPICC Identify pain field (23 Mar 2026)
metrics.narrative: No quantified ROI, savings, or cost figure has been put to the buyer. The pain is articulated qualitatively via the CRM MEDDPICC pain field (updated 23 Mar 2026): margin pressure post-insolvency, procurement inefficiency and hidden cost leakage, and tail/maverick spend leakage. The move is to build a dollarized savings case for Shaheen De Vries before the decision is finalized.

champion.narrative: EY South Africa team is the primary champion by CRM record, fronting the entire engagement end to end ('EY will front', 2 Mar 2026) and remaining the sole active channel through the 1 Jul 2026 status-check. Internally, Zeyn Khan (Strategic Sourcing) and Zuko Ndima (Contracts) are flagged as potential champions from the Feb 2026 demo period, but no direct advocacy action (an introduction, a shared internal document, pushback on SpotDraft) has been evidenced since. The champion picture is developing and at risk because Zycus has no confirmed internal Cell C advocate independent of EY.

competition.narrative: Coupa was in the evaluation and withdrew 30 Mar 2026 ('Coupa puled out of this one - we have a fair chance'). The CRM MEDDPICC Competition field (23 Mar 2026) separately names SpotDraft, a CLM point-solution, as a rival for the Contract Lifecycle Management scope of RFP 2026-005; no call evidence has corroborated or refreshed SpotDraft's standing since. The wedge, if SpotDraft is still live, is that Zycus offers the full source-to-pay suite (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing) against a narrower CLM-only competitor.

sources: SF MEDDPICC Identify pain field (23 Mar 2026)
identify_pain.narrative: The CRM MEDDPICC pain field (23 Mar 2026, EY-relayed context) names five aligned pains: financial turnaround pressure and margin erosion post-insolvency, lack of spend visibility under Cell C's capex-light/MVNO vendor-dependent model, tail/maverick spend leakage, compliance and audit-readiness risk tied to the JSE listing, and manual/fragmented procurement processes slowing decisions. This is EY-relayed, not a direct Avoma quote (the one matched call was not recorded), so treat the specifics as corroborated context rather than a verbatim buyer statement.

sources: No evidence found; inference from stage
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec) have surfaced yet, which is normal at this pre-vendor-selection stage. Visibility would come from asking EY what Cell C's standard paper process looks like once a preferred vendor is named, given the JSE listing mandate may add board-level sign-off.

sources: Avoma manifest, 8 May 2026 meeting attendee list
economic_buyer.narrative: Shaheen De Vries, Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement, is the named Opportunity Contact Role and attended the 8 May 2026 orals/demo (confirmed via the Avoma attendee list, s.devries@cellc.co.za), though that meeting was not recorded so no direct quote from her exists. There has been no confirmed direct contact with her since 8 May; Zycus is relying on EY to relay her position, which is a real engagement gap even though the role is identified.

decision_process.narrative: The process is well mapped: short intro 27 Jan 2026 as one of multiple vendors via EY, Agentic AI demo 16 Feb and Telco-specific follow-up demo 24 Feb 2026 ('Cell C liked our solution'), EY-led discussion 2 Mar, RFP deadline 16 Mar with EY support, Coupa's withdrawal 30 Mar, orals and demo 8 May, and extra questions answered 29 May. EY explicitly told Zycus to expect a preferred-vendor appointment within a month of the 8 May session; that milestone has slipped without a stated new date.

decision_criteria.narrative: Formal criteria were set by RFP 2026-005 (Contract Lifecycle Management Solution), submitted 16 Mar 2026 with EY support, followed by an orals-and-demo evaluation round on 8 May 2026 and a written round of extra questions answered 29 May 2026. The specific scoring rubric has not been shared with Zycus; what is confirmed is the process shape (RFP -> orals/demo -> Q&A), not the weighting.

engagement.top_event: [Clari - Email Received] Re: EY - RFP 2026-005 CONTRACT LIFECYCLE MANAGEMENT SOLUTION

items.name: Close date pushed 30 days
items.summary: Close date moved from 2026-07-31 to 2026-08-31 with no accompanying note — a slip that suggests the deal has stalled or hit a snag, but the reason isn't documented here.

day_summary.overall: The only thing that happened today was a quiet pushback of the close date by a month, from end of July to end of August — no calls, emails, or notes accompanying it. That's a signal the deal isn't tracking to plan, though without any other activity logged, we can't tell whether it's a buyer-driven delay or just an internal reforecast. Ball is effectively in our court to find out why and re-engage.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 12 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 33 is carried by exec access strength +1.00 (weight 15); differentiation strength +0.60 (weight 20) — from MEDDPICC: Critical pains clearly aligned to…. Offsetting it: momentum 45 vs stage-expected 52.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 45 (roughly flat): last genuine buyer touch 42d ago vs the ~21d cadence this stage needs — going quiet. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 5 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.38).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 50 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 4 open commitment(s); Direct economic-buyer access recorded.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 27 is anchored on the stage win (33), nudged by commitment 50 and momentum 45, and dragged by risk 12. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

items.detail: The one-month decision window EY forecast (by early June) has elapsed with no preferred-vendor announcement, and the buyer has been silent for 41 days since the last email reply (27 May). Rep outreach 1 Jul to EY for an update has yielded no reply (6 days and counting). Close date 31 Aug is 55 days out, leaving limited runway if the decision drags further or requires additional approvals.

Cell C is a post-insolvency turnaround case (implied budget constraints and cost-sensitivity), and the 41-day silence may signal budget freeze, internal funding approval complexity, or prioritization of more urgent operational spend over a $150k S2P initiative. No quantified ROI or business case has been articulated by the buyer, leaving the investment justification unanchored.

Shaheen De Vries (Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement) is the named economic buyer, but her engagement strength and sponsorship level are unclear (attended 8 May demo/orals but meeting not recorded; no direct contact since). Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct internal Cell C champion engagement. If EY's influence is weaker than assumed, or if Shaheen or another Cell C executive objects, the deal stalls.

IPO readiness and board approval complexity: Cell C's JSE listing mandate may require board-level or executive-committee approval for a $150k procurement investment, and the 41-day silence may indicate the decision is escalated beyond Shaheen De Vries or EY's recommendation. No visibility into whether additional approvals are required or whether the IPO timeline has shifted, deprioritizing the S2P initiative.
EY's forecast of a preferred-vendor appointment within a month of the 8 May 2026 orals has elapsed by roughly five weeks with no announcement.
The named economic buyer, Shaheen De Vries, has no confirmed direct engagement or recorded sentiment since the 8 May 2026 orals; Zycus is reliant on EY relaying her position.
No quantified business case exists to support a $150k spend for a post-insolvency, cost-sensitive buyer undergoing IPO-readiness scrutiny.
Post-insolvency financial turnaround pressure and no quantified ROI could make a new $150k spend harder to justify to Cell C's committee.
Cell C's JSE listing/IPO-readiness governance may require board-level approval not yet confirmed for this spend.
Zycus has no confirmed direct read on Cell C's internal sentiment since the 8 May orals and is relying entirely on EY to relay updates.
EY's forecast of a preferred-vendor decision by early June 2026 is now roughly a month overdue with no announcement.

critical_signals.text: Coupa was eliminated from the RFP in late March, leaving SpotDraft as the only other named rival, though nothing has confirmed its current standing since March.
The 8 May orals and demo went well enough that a preferred-vendor decision was expected within a month, but that meeting was never recorded and no announcement has followed two months later.
Marius Potgieter, Senior Manager of Property and Procurement, sits on the contact roles but has never appeared in a call or a next-step note, so his stance is unread.
No pricing or ROI conversation has happened yet, leaving the $150,000 ask unquantified for a buyer under post-insolvency cost pressure.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled since the 29 May written Q&A exchange; the 1 Jul status-check to EY has gone unanswered for 6 days as of today, and EY's early-June decision forecast has slipped by roughly a month.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 13 Feb 2026 (144 days in-cycle as of 7 Jul). RFP submitted 16 Mar 2026; orals/demo delivered 8 May 2026 (~60 days ago), after which EY forecast a preferred-vendor decision 'within a month' (~by 8 Jun 2026) - that forecast is now ~29 days overdue. 55 days remain to the 31 Aug 2026 close date, leaving a tight runway to reach paper if the decision lands soon.

Next_Step__c 30/03/2026: 'Coupa puled out of this one - we have a fair chance'
north_star_verdict.headline: Stalled at the finish line: EY's early-June preferred-vendor call is roughly a month overdue and the 1 Jul 2026 status-check has gone unanswered for 6 days.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (current category is already appropriately conservative given the overdue decision and no confirmed direct buyer read since 8 May)

flags: Multi-thread inside Cell C beyond EY: Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct engagement from internal Cell C stakeholders (Zeyn Khan, Zuko Ndima, Shaheen De Vries all confirmed in roles but engagement strength unclear). Risk: if EY's influence is weaker than assumed or an internal Cell C exec objects, the deal stalls. High-leverage move: secure a direct 1:1 with Shaheen De Vries (the economic buyer) to confirm her sponsorship and diagnose any internal hesitation, independently of EY's fronting role.

Reframe the business case as an IPO-readiness enabler: Cell C's JSE listing mandate requires audit-ready procurement (a regulatory forcing function), but no quantified ROI or savings case has been articulated. Re-engage with a compliance-led narrative (S2P = de-risk the IPO by delivering audit-grade spend visibility and controls) and quantify the value (e.g. tail-spend reduction %, contract-cycle compression, compliance-audit cost avoidance) to justify the investment in a post-insolvency budget environment.

Offer a phased or cost-optimized commercial structure: Given Cell C's post-insolvency turnaround context and the 40-day silence (possible budget sensitivity), propose a Phase 1 focused on compliance/audit readiness (iContract + iSource) with deferred Phase 2 for eProcurement/eInvoicing, or explore a term-extended payment structure to ease cash-flow impact. This lowers the entry barrier and aligns with their capital-light MVNO model while still delivering the IPO-readiness mandate.

EY-mediated engagement has gone silent for 41 days (last contact 27 May, no reply to 1 Jul outreach). Zycus is over-reliant on the external fronting partner with no direct Cell C internal champion actively driving the decision. Secure a direct call with Shaheen De Vries (EB) to validate budget, timeline, and decision authority, bypassing the EY bottleneck.

Decision Maker and approval chain unclear. Shaheen De Vries (EB) may require CFO, board, or exec-committee sign-off for a material procurement investment in the IPO context, but no visibility into the approval process. Map the final signatory and approval gates before the close date tightens further.
Build a quantified business case now (cost/margin impact) to give EY and Shaheen De Vries ammunition for an internal sign-off, since no numbers have been articulated to date.
Convert Zeyn Khan and Zuko Ndima from passive, previously-positive contacts into active internal champions who can independently confirm status while EY is silent.
Coupa's March withdrawal opened the field; confirm SpotDraft's current status and get a CLM-vs-S2P differentiation note to EY before the decision closes.
best_practice_check.summary: Three levers stand out to restart momentum before the decision is finalized.

SpotDraft is the only other named rival, per the CRM MEDDPICC Competition field updated 23 Mar 2026, but no call evidence since has confirmed whether it remains active in the evaluation; treat it as the current strongest known threat only because it is the sole one still unresolved.

hard.ais_why:
• Product scope is traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iSource, iSupplier) plus ML iAnalyze ‚Äî some AI/ML in analytics but no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no Merlin Intake selected. ML iAnalyze is the sole AI SKU and it's a spend-analytics product (machine learning categorisation) rather than the generative-AI/agentic stack. AI is not the headline of the deal.
• Formal Evaluation stage with EY South Africa engaged as advisor (jakob.schleu@za.ey.com, EY India team in support) and a strong Cell C buying committee including s.devries, lkekae, zndima and rfourie. Three meetings scheduled Feb-Mar 2026 but all three returned 'not_recorded' state in Avoma, so we cannot verify any customer-spoken AI sentiment from transcripts. BD_Engagement is Engaged per SFDC.
• Low confidence: SF Description is null and all Avoma meetings unrecorded. The score rests on product mix (ML iAnalyze hints at analytics AI) plus EY's involvement as an advisory partner. Default DOWN at 5/6 boundary held given the complete lack of explicit AI evidence from transcripts. Would move UP only if a recorded discovery surfaces buyer-side AI demand or RFP issuance specifies AI capability.

deltas.from: The one-month decision window EY forecast (by early June) has elapsed with no preferred-vendor announcement, and the buyer has been silent for 40 days since the

Close date 31 Aug is 56 days out, leaving limited runway if the decision drags further or requires additional approvals.

deltas.subject: Cell C is a post-insolvency turnaround case (implied budget constraints and cost-sensitivity), and the 40-day silence may signal budget freeze, internal funding approval complexity, or prioritization of more urgent operational spend over a $150k S2P initiative. No quantified ROI or business case has been articulated by the buyer, leaving the investment justification unanchored.

deltas.to: Shaheen De Vries (Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement) is the named economic buyer, but her engagement strength and sponsorship level are unclear (atte
deltas.from: Shaheen De Vries (Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement) is the named economic buyer, but her engagement strength and sponsorship level are unclear (atte
deltas.subject: Shaheen De Vries (Executive: Operational Finance & Procurement) is the named economic buyer, but her engagement strength and sponsorship level are unclear (attended 8 May demo/orals but meeting not recorded; no direct contact since). Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct internal Cell C champion engagement. If EY's influence is weaker than assumed, or if Shaheen or another Cell C executive objects, the deal stalls.

deltas.subject: IPO readiness and board approval complexity: Cell C's JSE listing mandate may require board-level or executive-committee approval for a $150k procurement investment, and the 40-day silence may indicate the decision is escalated beyond Shaheen De Vries or EY's recommendation. No visibility into whether additional approvals are required or whether the IPO timeline has shifted, deprioritizing the S2P initiative.

deltas.to: EY-mediated engagement has gone silent for 41 days (last contact 27 May, no reply to 1 Jul outreach). Zycus is over-reliant on the external fronting partner wit
deltas.subject: EY-mediated engagement has gone silent for 41 days (last contact 27 May, no reply to 1 Jul outreach). Zycus is over-reliant on the external fronting partner with no direct Cell C internal champion actively driving the decision. Secure a direct call with Shaheen De Vries (EB) to validate budget, timeline, and decision authority, bypassing the EY bottleneck.

deltas.to: No quantified ROI or business case anchors the $150k investment to Cell C's post-insolvency margin-improvement mandate. Co-author a one-page savings/efficiency
deltas.subject: No quantified ROI or business case anchors the $150k investment to Cell C's post-insolvency margin-improvement mandate. Co-author a one-page savings/efficiency model with Shaheen or the procurement lead (Zeyn Khan) tying Zycus S2P to measurable cost reduction, audit readiness, and IPO governance value.

deltas.to: Decision Maker and approval chain unclear. Shaheen De Vries (EB) may require CFO, board, or exec-committee sign-off for a material procurement investment in the
deltas.subject: Decision Maker and approval chain unclear. Map the final signatory and approval gates before the close date tightens further.

deltas.to: Multi-thread inside Cell C beyond EY: Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct engagement from internal Cell C stakeholders (Ze
deltas.subject: Multi-thread inside Cell C beyond EY: Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct engagement from internal Cell C stakeholders (Zeyn Khan, Zuko Ndima, Shaheen De Vries all confirmed in roles but engagement strength unclear). Risk: if EY's influence is weaker than assumed or an internal Cell C exec objects, the deal stalls. High-leverage move: secure a direct 1:1 with Shaheen De Vries (the economic buyer) to confirm her sponsorship and diagnose any internal hesitation, independently of EY's fronting role.

deltas.to: Reframe the business case as an IPO-readiness enabler: Cell C's JSE listing mandate requires audit-ready procurement (a regulatory forcing function), but no qua
deltas.subject: Reframe the business case as an IPO-readiness enabler: Cell C's JSE listing mandate requires audit-ready procurement (a regulatory forcing function), but no quantified ROI or savings case has been articulated. Re-engage with a compliance-led narrative (S2P = de-risk the IPO by delivering audit-grade spend visibility and controls) and quantify the value (e.g. tail-spend reduction %, contract-cycle compression, compliance-audit cost avoidance) to justify the investment in a post-insolvency budget environment.

deltas.to: Offer a phased or cost-optimized commercial structure: Given Cell C's post-insolvency turnaround context and the 40-day silence (possible budget sensitivity), p
deltas.subject: Offer a phased or cost-optimized commercial structure: Given Cell C's post-insolvency turnaround context and the 40-day silence (possible budget sensitivity), propose a Phase 1 focused on compliance/audit readiness (iContract + iSource) with deferred Phase 2 for eProcurement/eInvoicing, or explore a term-extended payment structure to ease cash-flow impact. This lowers the entry barrier and aligns with their capital-light MVNO model while still delivering the IPO-readiness mandate.

packets.subject: IPO readiness and board approval complexity: Cell C's JSE listing mandate may require board-level or executive-committee approval for a $150k procurement investment, and the 40-day silence may indicate the decision is escalated beyond Shaheen De Vries or EY's recommendation. value.flag: Multi-thread inside Cell C beyond EY: Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct engagement from internal Cell C stakeholders (Zeyn Khan, Zuko Ndima, Shaheen De Vries all confirmed in roles but engagement strength unclear). Risk: if EY's influence is weaker than assumed or an internal Cell C exec objects, the deal stalls. High-leverage move: secure a direct 1:1 with Shaheen De Vries (the economic buyer) to confirm her sponsorship and diagnose any internal hesitation, independently of EY's fronting role.

packets.subject: Multi-thread inside Cell C beyond EY: Zycus is heavily reliant on EY's external championing, with weaker direct engagement from internal Cell C stakeholders (Zeyn Khan, Zuko Ndima, Shaheen De Vries all confirmed in roles but engagement strength unclear). Risk: if EY's influence is weaker than assumed or an internal Cell C exec objects, the deal stalls. value.flag: Reframe the business case as an IPO-readiness enabler: Cell C's JSE listing mandate requires audit-ready procurement (a regulatory forcing function), but no quantified ROI or savings case has been articulated. Re-engage with a compliance-led narrative (S2P = de-risk the IPO by delivering audit-grade spend visibility and controls) and quantify the value (e.g. tail-spend reduction %, contract-cycle compression, compliance-audit cost avoidance) to justify the investment in a post-insolvency budget environment.

packets.subject: Reframe the business case as an IPO-readiness enabler: Cell C's JSE listing mandate requires audit-ready procurement (a regulatory forcing function), but no quantified ROI or savings case has been articulated. value.flag: Offer a phased or cost-optimized commercial structure: Given Cell C's post-insolvency turnaround context and the 40-day silence (possible budget sensitivity), propose a Phase 1 focused on compliance/audit readiness (iContract + iSource) with deferred Phase 2 for eProcurement/eInvoicing, or explore a term-extended payment structure to ease cash-flow impact. This lowers the entry barrier and aligns with their capital-light MVNO model while still delivering the IPO-readiness mandate.
